title: croton longipedicellatus subsp. longipedicellatus
intro: Croton longipedicellatus subsp. longipedicellatus, commonly known as the Long Pedicellate Croton, is a species of flowering plant in the spurge family (Euphorbiaceae). It is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and soothing digestive issues, as well as potentially lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
